texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Development   of   large-area   DDR-type   zeolite   membranes
texts.text: Development   of   large-area   DDR-type   zeolite   membranes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Kenji   Yajima,   Takeshi   Hagio,   Makoto   Miyahara,   Naoko   Takahashi, Makiko   Niino,   Manabu   Isomura,   and   Shuichi   Yoshida.
texts.text: Kenji   Yajima,   Takeshi   Hagio,   Makoto   Miyahara,   Naoko   Takahashi, Makiko   Niino,   Manabu   Isomura,   and   Shuichi   Yoshida.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: NGK   INSULATORS,   LTD.   NCM   Project
texts.text: NGK   INSULATORS,   LTD.   NCM   Project

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We   have   succeeded   in   making   a   DDR-type   zeolite   membrane   for   the   first   time   in   the   world,   and   confirmed   that   it   has   high   separation performance   from   the   results   of   permeation   performance   measurements   of   various   gases.   We   have   developed   a   monolith-type   large-area DDR-type   zeolite   membrane   with   a   diameter   of   180   mm,   a   total   length   of   1000   mm,   and   a   membrane   area   of   12   m2 .   The   purpose   of   making it   large   is   to   handle   huge   amounts   of   processing,   such   as   natural   gas   refining.   The   carbon   dioxide   permeation   rate   and   carbon   dioxide/ methane   separation   factor   at   a   supply   gas   pressure   of   0.3   MPaG   and   a   50/50   carbon   dioxide/methane   mixture   are   high   at   5×10-7   mol/m2   s Pa   and   over   160,   respectively,   and   are   considered   to   be   at   a   level   that   can   be   applied   to   natural   gas   refining.   In   addition,   it   is   possible   to separate   hydrogen   from   a   hydrogen/methane   mixture   and   carbon   dioxide   from   carbon   dioxide/hydrogen.   In   addition,   our   DDR   membrane has   excellent   acid   resistance   because   it   contains   almost   no   aluminum.   When   applied   to   the   dehydration   of   organic   solvents,   it   has   been confirmed   that   high   separation   performance   is   maintained   for   more   than   200   hours   in   tests   using   the   pervaporation   method   at   an   acetic   acid concentration   of   90   wt%   and   a   temperature   of   90°C. In   addition   to   acetic   acid,   the   high   separation   performance   is   also   demonstrated   in   the dehydration   of   phenols,   esters,   and   alcohols,   so   a   wide   range   of   applications   is   expected.
texts.text: We   have   succeeded   in   making   a   DDR-type   zeolite   membrane   for   the   first   time   in   the   world,   and   confirmed   that   it   has   high   separation performance   from   the   results   of   permeation   performance   measurements   of   various   gases.   We   have   developed   a   monolith-type   large-area DDR-type   zeolite   membrane   with   a   diameter   of   180   mm,   a   total   length   of   1000   mm,   and   a   membrane   area   of   12   m2 .   The   purpose   of   making it   large   is   to   handle   huge   amounts   of   processing,   such   as   natural   gas   refining.   The   carbon   dioxide   permeation   rate   and   carbon   dioxide/ methane   separation   factor   at   a   supply   gas   pressure   of   0.3   MPaG   and   a   50/50   carbon   dioxide/methane   mixture   are   high   at   5×10-7   mol/m2   s Pa   and   over   160,   respectively,   and   are   considered   to   be   at   a   level   that   can   be   applied   to   natural   gas   refining.   In   addition,   it   is   possible   to separate   hydrogen   from   a   hydrogen/methane   mixture   and   carbon   dioxide   from   carbon   dioxide/hydrogen.   In   addition,   our   DDR   membrane has   excellent   acid   resistance   because   it   contains   almost   no   aluminum.   When   applied   to   the   dehydration   of   organic   solvents,   it   has   been confirmed   that   high   separation   performance   is   maintained   for   more   than   200   hours   in   tests   using   the   pervaporation   method   at   an   acetic   acid concentration   of   90   wt%   and   a   temperature   of   90°C. In   addition   to   acetic   acid,   the   high   separation   performance   is   also   demonstrated   in   the dehydration   of   phenols,   esters,   and   alcohols,   so   a   wide   range   of   applications   is   expected.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1.   Background
texts.text: 1.   Background

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We   have   succeeded   in   making   DDR-type   zeolite   membranes   for   the   first time   in   the   world,   and   have   confirmed   that   it   has   high   separation   performance from   the   results   of   measurements   of   the   permeability   of   various   gases.1)   We have   now   developed   a   large-area   monolithic   DDR-type   zeolite   membrane   with a   diameter   of   180   mm   and   a   total   length   of   1000   mm,   and   we   will   describe   its features,   the   purpose   of   the   large   area,   and   gas   separation   performance. Furthermore,   we   will   introduce   the   expectations   for   DDR-type   zeolite membranes   and   various   dehydration   performances   for   their   application   to   dehydration.
texts.text: We   have   succeeded   in   making   DDR-type   zeolite   membranes   for   the   first time   in   the   world,   and   have   confirmed   that   it   has   high   separation   performance from   the   results   of   measurements   of   the   permeability   of   various   gases.1)   We have   now   developed   a   large-area   monolithic   DDR-type   zeolite   membrane   with a   diameter   of   180   mm   and   a   total   length   of   1000   mm,   and   we   will   describe   its features,   the   purpose   of   the   large   area,   and   gas   separation   performance. Furthermore,   we   will   introduce   the   expectations   for   DDR-type   zeolite membranes   and   various   dehydration   performances   for   their   application   to   dehydration.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1.1   Characteristics   of   DDR-type   zeolite   membranes
texts.text: 1.1   Characteristics   of   DDR-type   zeolite   membranes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: First,   the   characteristics   of   DDR-type   zeolite   membranes   are introduced.   DDR-type   zeolite   (DDR)   was   first   synthesized   by   Gies   in   the 1980s   and   has   two-dimensional   elliptical   pores   of   0.36   ×   0.44   nm consisting   of   eight-membered   oxygen   rings.
texts.text: First,   the   characteristics   of   DDR-type   zeolite   membranes   are introduced.   DDR-type   zeolite   (DDR)   was   first   synthesized   by   Gies   in   the 1980s   and   has   two-dimensional   elliptical   pores   of   0.36   ×   0.44   nm consisting   of   eight-membered   oxygen   rings.

texts.orig: It   has   been   reported   that   DDR   has   a   high   separation   performance   when made   into   a   membrane   due   to   the   uniform   pores   in   the   crystal.
texts.text: It   has   been   reported   that   DDR   has   a   high   separation   performance   when made   into   a   membrane   due   to   the   uniform   pores   in   the   crystal.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The   DDR   membrane   is   a   high-silica   composition   that   contains   almost no   aluminum,   so   it   is   more   chemically   stable   and   has   excellent   resistance to   water   vapor   and   acid   compared   to   general   zeolites   that   contain aluminum.   Figure   2   shows the   results   of   an   investigation   into   the   permeability   of   various   gases using   a   small   monolithic   DDR   membrane   element   with   a   diameter   of   30 mm   and   a   total   length   of   160   mm.   The   permeability   of   gases   is   low   for hydrocarbons   that   are   larger   than   the   pores   of   the   DDR,   and   high   for   other   gases.
texts.text: The   DDR   membrane   is   a   high-silica   composition   that   contains   almost no   aluminum,   so   it   is   more   chemically   stable   and   has   excellent   resistance to   water   vapor   and   acid   compared   to   general   zeolites   that   contain aluminum.   Figure   2   shows the   results   of   an   investigation   into   the   permeability   of   various   gases using   a   small   monolithic   DDR   membrane   element   with   a   diameter   of   30 mm   and   a   total   length   of   160   mm.   The   permeability   of   gases   is   low   for hydrocarbons   that   are   larger   than   the   pores   of   the   DDR,   and   high   for   other   gases.

texts.orig: Figure   3.   Example   of   application:   Natural   gas   refining   (CO2   separation)
texts.text: Figure   3.   Example   of   application:   Natural   gas   refining   (CO2   separation)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The   smaller   carbon   dioxide,   hydrogen,   and   helium   have   high   separation efficiency,   which   shows   that   they   can   be   separated   by   the   molecular   sieve mechanism.   For   example,   high   separation   performance   can   be   obtained even   with   a   mixture   of   carbon   dioxide   and   methane,   and   an   example   of   its application   is   carbon   dioxide   separation   in   natural   gas   refining,   as   shown   in Figure   3.   Gas   produced   from   gas   fields   may   contain   a   large   amount   of unnecessary   carbon   dioxide,   which   must   be   removed   to   a   certain concentration   before   commercialization.   When   the   carbon   dioxide concentration   is   relatively   low,   carbon   dioxide   can   be   separated   with   high accuracy   using   amine   absorption,   but   when   the   carbon   dioxide   concentration is   high,   the   problem   is   that   the   amount   of   processing   increases   and   energy costs   become   high.   On   the   other   hand,   since   the   pressure   difference   is   the driving   force   for   separation   in   separation   membranes,   carbon   dioxide   can be   separated   at   low   energy   costs   when   natural   gas   is   originally   produced at   high   pressure.   By   using   separation   membranes   in   combination   with   the amine   absorption   method,   it   is   possible   to   save   more   energy   than   before.
texts.text: The   smaller   carbon   dioxide,   hydrogen,   and   helium   have   high   separation efficiency,   which   shows   that   they   can   be   separated   by   the   molecular   sieve mechanism.   For   example,   high   separation   performance   can   be   obtained even   with   a   mixture   of   carbon   dioxide   and   methane,   and   an   example   of   its application   is   carbon   dioxide   separation   in   natural   gas   refining,   as   shown   in Figure   3.   Gas   produced   from   gas   fields   may   contain   a   large   amount   of unnecessary   carbon   dioxide,   which   must   be   removed   to   a   certain concentration   before   commercialization.   When   the   carbon   dioxide concentration   is   relatively   low,   carbon   dioxide   can   be   separated   with   high accuracy   using   amine   absorption,   but   when   the   carbon   dioxide   concentration is   high,   the   problem   is   that   the   amount   of   processing   increases   and   energy costs   become   high.   On   the   other   hand,   since   the   pressure   difference   is   the driving   force   for   separation   in   separation   membranes,   carbon   dioxide   can be   separated   at   low   energy   costs   when   natural   gas   is   originally   produced at   high   pressure.   By   using   separation   membranes   in   combination   with   the amine   absorption   method,   it   is   possible   to   save   more   energy   than   before.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The   requirements   for   membranes   in   such   applications   include low   methane   leakage,   high   carbon   dioxide   permeability,   high pressure   resistance,   and   resistance   to   high   concentrations   of carbon   dioxide.   DDR   membranes   meet   these   requirements,   and are   therefore   considered   to   be   one   of   the   most   promising   applications. is.
texts.text: The   requirements   for   membranes   in   such   applications   include low   methane   leakage,   high   carbon   dioxide   permeability,   high pressure   resistance,   and   resistance   to   high   concentrations   of carbon   dioxide.   DDR   membranes   meet   these   requirements,   and are   therefore   considered   to   be   one   of   the   most   promising   applications. is.

texts.orig: Figure   4.   Ceramic   membrane   products
texts.text: Figure   4.   Ceramic   membrane   products

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1.2   Purpose   of   increasing   area
texts.text: 1.2   Purpose   of   increasing   area

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When   commercializing   membranes   for   applications   such   as natural   gas   refining,   a   large   membrane   area   is   required   to   handle a   huge   amount   of   processing.   This   increases   the   volume, installation   area,   and   weight   of   the   equipment,   and   the   number of   membranes   increases,   which   increases   the   number   of connections   between   the   membrane   and   the   module,   so-called seal   parts,   which   increases   the   risk   of   seal   leakage.   Therefore, we   selected   a   monolithic   type   that   leads   to   a   compact   design and   a   large   shape.   As   shown   in   Figure   4,   we   manufacture ceramic   membranes   of   different   sizes   for   various   separation applications.   We   improved   the   ceramic   membrane   for   water purification   plants,   which   is   the   largest   among   them,   and   decided to   use   it   as   the   substrate   for   the   DDR   membrane.   The   shape   is 180   mm   in   diameter,   1000   mm   in   total   length,   and   the   membrane area   is   12   m2 ,   which   is   equivalent   to   about   400   tubular   membranes   with   a   diame do.
texts.text: When   commercializing   membranes   for   applications   such   as natural   gas   refining,   a   large   membrane   area   is   required   to   handle a   huge   amount   of   processing.   This   increases   the   volume, installation   area,   and   weight   of   the   equipment,   and   the   number of   membranes   increases,   which   increases   the   number   of connections   between   the   membrane   and   the   module,   so-called seal   parts,   which   increases   the   risk   of   seal   leakage.   Therefore, we   selected   a   monolithic   type   that   leads   to   a   compact   design and   a   large   shape.   As   shown   in   Figure   4,   we   manufacture ceramic   membranes   of   different   sizes   for   various   separation applications.   We   improved   the   ceramic   membrane   for   water purification   plants,   which   is   the   largest   among   them,   and   decided to   use   it   as   the   substrate   for   the   DDR   membrane.   The   shape   is 180   mm   in   diameter,   1000   mm   in   total   length,   and   the   membrane area   is   12   m2 ,   which   is   equivalent   to   about   400   tubular   membranes   with   a   diame do.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.   Large-area   DDR   film   2.1 Manufacturing   method
texts.text: 2.   Large-area   DDR   film   2.1 Manufacturing   method

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The   basic   manufacturing   process   for   DDR   films   is   shown   in   Figure   5.   It consists   of   three   main   steps:   seeding,   hydrothermal   synthesis,   and   template removal.   DDR   particles   that   serve   as   seed   crystals   are   attached   to   the substrate   surface,   the   seeds   are   grown   in   solution   to   form   a   film,   and   finally the   organic   template   is   removed   by   burning.   To   achieve   a   large   area,   three important   issues   were:   reducing   coarse   defects   on   the   substrate   surface   by optimizing   the   particle   size   of   the   substrate   constituent   particles,   uniform seeding   on   the   substrate   surface   by   optimizing   the   seed   particle   size   and coating   density,   and   controlling   the   variation   in   film   thickness   by   optimizing the   sol   composition   and   heating   conditions   in   the   hydrothermal   synthesis.
texts.text: The   basic   manufacturing   process   for   DDR   films   is   shown   in   Figure   5.   It consists   of   three   main   steps:   seeding,   hydrothermal   synthesis,   and   template removal.   DDR   particles   that   serve   as   seed   crystals   are   attached   to   the substrate   surface,   the   seeds   are   grown   in   solution   to   form   a   film,   and   finally the   organic   template   is   removed   by   burning.   To   achieve   a   large   area,   three important   issues   were:   reducing   coarse   defects   on   the   substrate   surface   by optimizing   the   particle   size   of   the   substrate   constituent   particles,   uniform seeding   on   the   substrate   surface   by   optimizing   the   seed   particle   size   and coating   density,   and   controlling   the   variation   in   film   thickness   by   optimizing the   sol   composition   and   heating   conditions   in   the   hydrothermal   synthesis.

texts.orig: However,   since   the   data   are   not   identical,   a   strict   comparison   cannot   be   made. Our   DDR   film   has   high   performance   in   terms   of   both   the   separation   coefficient   and   the   surface   roughness. The   light-based   membranes   have   a   higher   permeation   rate   and   separation   ability   than   polymer   membranes. It   has   a   large   surface   area,   high   permeation   rate   and   high   separation   coefficient. Figure   7   summarizes   the   separation   performance   of   polymer   membranes   and   other   inorganic   membranes. However,   it   exhibits   performance   comparable   to   other   zeolite   membranes. The   performance   is   plotted   in   the   graph   in   Figure   7. texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.   Application   to   dehydration   3.1
texts.text: 3.   Application   to   dehydration   3.1

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Expectations   for   DDR   membranes
texts.text: Expectations   for   DDR   membranes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The   use   of   DDR   membranes   is   not   limited   to   gases,   but   can   also   be used   to   separate   liquids.   In   particular,   the   pore   size   of   DDR   is   larger   than that   of   water   and   smaller   than   that   of   many   organic   solvents,   so   it   is believed   that   highly   accurate   dehydration   of   organic   solvents   is   possible. Currently,   in   chemical   processes,   distillation   is   used   to   separate   many organic   solvents   from   water,   but   Matsukata   7)   has   calculated   that   the use   of   membranes   in   combination   with   distillation   or   in   place   of   distillation can   be   expected   to   significantly   reduce   energy   consumption.   Figure   8   7) shows   a   graph   sorting   dehydration   needs   by   production   volume   and technical   difficulty   to   show   that   there   are   many   types   of   dehydration needs.   Among   these,   acetic   acid   has   the   highest   production   volume   and high   dehydration   needs,   but   it   also   has   the   highest   technical   difficulty   in terms   of   water   resistance,   heat   resistance,   and   acid   resistance,   indicating that   there   are   high   hurdles   to   practical   use.   However,   our   DDR   membrane has   a   high   silica   composition,   so   it   is   highly   chemically   stable   and   is compatible   with   general   aluminum   oxide.
texts.text: The   use   of   DDR   membranes   is   not   limited   to   gases,   but   can   also   be used   to   separate   liquids.   In   particular,   the   pore   size   of   DDR   is   larger   than that   of   water   and   smaller   than   that   of   many   organic   solvents,   so   it   is believed   that   highly   accurate   dehydration   of   organic   solvents   is   possible. Currently,   in   chemical   processes,   distillation   is   used   to   separate   many organic   solvents   from   water,   but   Matsukata   7)   has   calculated   that   the use   of   membranes   in   combination   with   distillation   or   in   place   of   distillation can   be   expected   to   significantly   reduce   energy   consumption.   Figure   8   7) shows   a   graph   sorting   dehydration   needs   by   production   volume   and technical   difficulty   to   show   that   there   are   many   types   of   dehydration needs.   Among   these,   acetic   acid   has   the   highest   production   volume   and high   dehydration   needs,   but   it   also   has   the   highest   technical   difficulty   in terms   of   water   resistance,   heat   resistance,   and   acid   resistance,   indicating that   there   are   high   hurdles   to   practical   use.   However,   our   DDR   membrane has   a   high   silica   composition,   so   it   is   highly   chemically   stable   and   is compatible   with   general   aluminum   oxide.

texts.text: Since   this   membrane   is   expected   to   have   superior   water   and   acid   resistance   compared   to zeolite   membranes   that   contain   a   large   amount   of   acetic   acid,   we   believe   that   this   membrane may   also   be   applicable   to   the   dehydration   of   acetic   acid.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2   Dehydration   performance
texts.text: 3.2   Dehydration   performance

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It   was   confirmed   that   the   DDR   membrane   exhibits   high   separation   performance over   a   wide   concentration   range.   Figure   11   shows   the   results   of   a   continuous   test with   a   supply   acetic   acid   concentration   of   90   wt%,   a   temperature   of   90°C, and   a vacuum   pressure   of   50   torr   on   the   permeation   side.   The   separation   performance was   stable   even   after   more   than   200   hours,   confirming   its   applicability   to   acetic acid   dehydration.   From   the   above,   it   was   shown   that   our   DDR   membrane   has high   durability   even   in   the   acidic   range.   Table   2   shows   the To   confirm   the   possibility   of   applying   the   DDR   membrane   to   acetic   acid dehydration,   we   evaluated   the   dehydration   performance   using   a   small monolithic   DDR   membrane   with   a   diameter   of   30   mm   and   a   total   length   of   160 mm.   The   test   was   performed   using   the   pervaporation   (PV)   method   shown   in Figure   9.   Figure   10   shows   the   test   results   for   acetic   acid   dehydration.   The   test conditions   were:   supply   acetic   acid   concentration   40-90   wt%,   temperature 90°C,   and   permeation   side   vacuum   pressure   50   torr.   From   these   results,   we   concluded   that   our evaluation   results   for   organic   solvents   other   than   acetic   acid.   The   organic   acid was   acetic   acid   as   mentioned   above,   and   the   aromatics   were   phenol   and   ethylenediamine.
texts.text: It   was   confirmed   that   the   DDR   membrane   exhibits   high   separation   performance over   a   wide   concentration   range. The   separation   performance was   stable   even   after   more   than   200   hours,   confirming   its   applicability   to   acetic acid   dehydration.   From   the   above,   it   was   shown   that   our   DDR   membrane   has high   durability   even   in   the   acidic   range.   Table   2   shows   the To   confirm   the   possibility   of   applying   the   DDR   membrane   to   acetic   acid dehydration,   we   evaluated   the   dehydration   performance   using   a   small monolithic   DDR   membrane   with   a   diameter   of   30   mm   and   a   total   length   of   160 mm.   The   test   was   performed   using   the   pervaporation   (PV)   method   shown   in Figure   9.   Figure   10   shows   the   test   results   for   acetic   acid   dehydration.   The   test conditions   were:   supply   acetic   acid   concentration   40-90   wt%,   temperature 90°C,   and   permeation   side   vacuum   pressure   50   torr.   From   these   results,   we   concluded   that   our evaluation   results   for   organic   solvents   other   than   acetic   acid.   The   organic   acid was   acetic   acid   as   mentioned   above,   and   the   aromatics   were   phenol   and   ethylenediamine.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.   Summary
texts.text: 4.   Summary

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We   developed   a   large-area   monolithic   DDR   membrane.
texts.text: We   developed   a   large-area   monolithic   DDR   membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The   membrane   developed   enables   compact   equipment   design,
texts.text: The   membrane   developed   enables   compact   equipment   design,

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It   will   be   easier   to   deploy   it   in   large-scale   applications   such   as   natural   gas   refining.
texts.text: It   will   be   easier   to   deploy   it   in   large-scale   applications   such   as   natural   gas   refining.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In   addition,   the   DDR   membrane   is   highly   effective   for   dehydration.
texts.text: In   addition,   the   DDR   membrane   is   highly   effective   for   dehydration.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In   the   future,   we   plan   to   realize   a   large-area   substrate   suitable   for   dehydration   applications. It   was   confirmed   that   the   separator   has   excellent   separation   performance   and   durability. We   will   also   aim   to   develop   this   technology   and   apply   it   to   various   dehydration   processes. In   particular,   the   application   of   this   method   to   the   dehydration   of   acetic   acid,   for   which   there   is   a   high   demand,   was   demonstrated.
texts.text: In   the   future,   we   plan   to   realize   a   large-area   substrate   suitable   for   dehydration   applications. It   was   confirmed   that   the   separator   has   excellent   separation   performance   and   durability. We   will   also   aim   to   develop   this   technology   and   apply   it   to   various   dehydration   processes. In   particular,   the   application   of   this   method   to   the   dehydration   of   acetic   acid,   for   which   there   is   a   high   demand,   was   demonstrated.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: permeance   and   selectivity   of   ÿ5×10ÿ7   mol/m2   s   Pa   and   ÿ160,   respectively,   for   equimolar   mixtures   of   CO2   and CH4   at   a   total   feed   pressure   of   3   bars.   We   expect   that   this   high   performance   shall   be   promising   for   industrial applications.   Application   to   other   binary   systems   including   H2   separation   from   H2/CH4   and   CO2   separation   from CO2/H2   mixtures   was   also   confirmed   to   be   possible.   Another   beneficial   feature   of   our   DDR-type   zeolite   membrane is   its   significantly   low   aluminum   content.   In   general,   zeolites   with   lower   aluminum   content   are   more   chemically stable   and   thus   our   membrane   is   expected   to   have   high   acidic   resistance,   suitable   for   dehydration   applications. The   dehydration   performance   of   our   membrane   has   been   actually   tested   in   several   organic   solvents/ water   systems   using   pervaporation   method.   Our   membrane   showed   high   performance   and   excellent   stability   for more   than   200   hrs   in   dehydration   of   90%   acetic   acid   solution   at   90ÿ.   Enhanced   dehydration   performance   has   also been   observed   in   other   solvents   including   phenol,   ester   and   alcohol   solutions. NGK   have   first   prepared   DDR-type   zeolite   membranes,   and   has   since   revealed   its   molecular   sieving   effect through   measurements   of   single   gas   permeation   of   various   gas   species.   Recently,   we   have   succeeded   in   scaling up   this   DDR-type   zeolite   membrane   into   a   large   multichanneled   structure   with   a   diameter   and   length   of   180   mm and   1000   mm,   respectively,   realizing   a   membrane   area   of   12   m2   within   a   single   membrane.   This   membrane   was developed   for   aiming   large-scale   applications   with   high   throughput,   such   as   natural   gas   purification.   Taking   this application   for   example,   the   performance   of   our   membrane   is   discussed   through   the   separation   of   CO2   from   CO2/ CH4   mixtures,   which   is   a   reference   index   for   natural   gas   purification.   Our   membrane   shows   a   high   CO2
